name: PEFT (HuggingFace)
pricing_notes: Fully open source (Apache 2.0); no API costs; GPU compute is the cost

auth.notes: Python library; HuggingFace token optional (for pushing/pulling from HF Hub)

performance.notes: LoRA/QLoRA training 3-10x faster than full fine-tuning; 4-bit quantization enables fine-tuning on consumer GPUs

agent_readiness.notes: HuggingFace Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning library. Implements LoRA, QLoRA, IA3, prefix tuning, and other PEFT methods. Fine-tune 7B+ LLMs on single GPU via 4-bit quantization. Integrates with Transformers, TRL, and Accelerate. HuggingFace Hub native (push/load adapters). Standard choice for LLM fine-tuning in 2024-2026.

gotchas: GPU required — no meaningful CPU fine-tuning for LLMs
QLoRA quality slightly below full fine-tuning for very complex tasks
PEFT adapter compatibility varies by model architecture
best_when: Fine-tuning LLMs on domain-specific data with limited GPU budget
Creating task-specific adapters (LoRA) deployable on base model
RLHF/DPO training pipelines with TRL integration
Researchers experimenting with new fine-tuning methods
avoid_when: No GPU infrastructure (too slow for meaningful training)
When full fine-tuning quality is required (use DeepSpeed/FSDP instead)
summary: PEFT is HuggingFace's standard library for parameter-efficient LLM fine-tuning. Implements LoRA, QLoRA, IA3, prefix tuning, and more. QLoRA enables fine-tuning 7B+ models on consumer GPUs. Native HuggingFace Hub integration for sharing adapters. Standard choice for production LLM fine-tuning alongside TRL (for RLHF/DPO). Works seamlessly with Transformers and Accelerate.
